2606.21460.title: Evaluation of Small Language Models for Arabic Language Processing
2606.21460.paper_summary: This paper evaluates the performance of twelve Small Language Models (SLMs) on Arabic natural language processing tasks. The study introduces a benchmark of 240 Arabic test items distributed across eight domains and ten language skills, covering both comprehension-oriented and generation-oriented tasks. All models were evaluated under a controlled zero-shot setting using a standardized Arabic-only prompt template. Model responses were assessed through a multi-model LLM-as-a-judge framework involving GPT-4.1 Mini, Claude Haiku 4.5, and DeepSeek-Chat, with scores aggregated across judges and analyzed by task, skill, and model family. The results show that Gemma 3 (12B) achieved the highest overall score (4.548/5), followed by Aya and C4AI Command Arabic. The observed results suggest that model size alone does not explain Arabic SLM performance. Models with stronger Arabic alignment and more reliable instruction-following behavior tended to perform better across tasks. Common failure patterns among lower-performing models include prompt leakage, hallucination, language drift, incomplete generation, and weak task adherence. Overall, the benchmark provides a structured reference for evaluating compact Arabic language models and supports future work on efficient, reliable, and culturally appropriate Arabic AI systems.
2606.21460.paper_summary_zh: 這篇論文評估了十二個小型語言模型（SLMs）在阿拉伯語自然語言處理任務上的表現。研究引入了一個基準，包含240個阿拉伯語測試項目，分佈在八個領域和十種語言技能上，涵蓋了理解導向和生成導向的任務。所有模型在控制的零樣本設置下進行評估，使用標準化的阿拉伯語專用提示模板。模型的回應通過一個多模型的LLM作為評判框架進行評估，涉及GPT-4.1 Mini、Claude Haiku 4.5和DeepSeek-Chat，並根據評判者的評分進行匯總，按任務、技能和模型家族進行分析。結果顯示，Gemma 3（12B）獲得了最高的整體分數（4.548/5），其次是Aya和C4AI Command Arabic。觀察到的結果表明，僅僅模型大小並不能解釋阿拉伯語SLM的表現。具有更強阿拉伯語對齊和更可靠的指令遵循行為的模型在各項任務中表現更佳。表現較差的模型中常見的失敗模式包括提示洩漏、幻覺、語言漂移、不完整生成和薄弱的任務遵循。總體而言，這個基準為評估緊湊的阿拉伯語語言模型提供了一個結構化的參考，並支持未來在高效、可靠和文化適宜的阿拉伯語人工智慧系統上的工作。
2606.21460.author: Jumana Alsubhi et.al.
2606.21460.authors: Jumana Alsubhi, Ahmed Alhusayni, Abdulrahman Gharawi, Israa Hamdine, Alshaymaa Allahim, Lamees Alhumaid, Ahmad Shabana, Rafik Madani
